description: Add Abuse Graph, Reputation system, Anti-Spam, Rate Limiter, and Captcha Challenger to packages/security/src/. These form the trust and safety enforcement layer that works alongside the moderation ML classifiers.
steps: Step 1: Update packages/security/package.json (create if missing) - Ensure it has dependencies: zod, @quant/common (workspace:*), pino, ioredis. Add devDeps: vitest, typescript, @types/node. Add tsconfig.json and vitest.config.ts if missing.

Step 2: Create packages/security/src/core/abuse-graph.ts - AbuseGraph class that builds a graph of reports between users. Nodes are users, edges are reports. Implement simple community detection (connected components with density threshold) to detect sybil clusters. Methods: addReport(reporterId, targetId), detectSybilClusters() => SybilCluster[], getUserRisk(userId) => number. Use adjacency list representation.

Step 3: Create packages/security/src/core/reputation.ts - ReputationService class. Trust score per user based on: account age, reports ratio, verification level, activity volume, content quality. Methods: calculateReputation(userId, factors) => ReputationScore, updateReputation(userId, event), getReputation(userId). Define ReputationScore type with Zod.

Step 4: Create packages/security/src/core/anti-spam.ts - AntiSpamFilter class. Bayesian + feature-based classification for email/messages. Features: link count, caps ratio, keyword frequency, sender reputation, time patterns. Methods: classify(content: SpamInput) => SpamResult, train(samples: TrainingSample[]), getStats(). Uses Naive Bayes probability calculation.

Step 5: Create packages/security/src/core/configurable-rate-limiter.ts - ConfigurableRateLimiter class (extends beyond the existing SlidingWindowRateLimiter). Per-user, per-IP, per-action configurable rate limits. Redis-backed interface (uses RateLimitStore interface for DI). Methods: checkLimit(key, action, config) => RateLimitResult, configure(action, limits), getStatus(key). When limit exceeded, returns 429-compatible response info.

Step 6: Create packages/security/src/core/captcha-challenger.ts - CaptchaChallengerService class. Triggers captcha when reputation low + suspicious activity. Methods: shouldChallenge(userId, activity) => ChallengeDecision, verifyCaptcha(token) => boolean, getChallengePending(userId). Integrates with reputation score threshold.

Step 7: Update packages/security/src/types.ts - Add all new types: AbuseReport, SybilCluster, ReputationScore, ReputationFactors, SpamInput, SpamResult, TrainingSample, RateLimitStore, ChallengeDecision, etc. All with Zod schemas.
Step 8: Update packages/security/src/index.ts barrel - Export all new modules: AbuseGraph, ReputationService, AntiSpamFilter, ConfigurableRateLimiter, CaptchaChallengerService.

Step 9: Create test files - packages/security/src/core/abuse-graph.test.ts (test sybil cluster detection with synthetic graph), reputation.test.ts (spammer score decreases as reports accumulate), anti-spam.test.ts (classifies test samples correctly), configurable-rate-limiter.test.ts (exceeding limit returns 429 info), captcha-challenger.test.ts (triggers when reputation low).
Step 10: Run pnpm install and verify: NODE_OPTIONS="" npx vitest run packages/security
acceptance_criteria: Abuse graph detects synthetic sybil cluster in test (group of users mutually reporting outsiders)
Reputation: spammer score decreases as reports accumulate
Anti-spam: classifies known spam/ham test samples correctly (>80% accuracy on test set)
Rate limiter: exceeding configured limit returns 429-compatible result
Captcha challenger: triggers challenge when reputation below threshold AND suspicious activity
Zod schemas for all config/input types
verification: Run: NODE_OPTIONS="" npx vitest run packages/security
